cases.input: We have a 300,000 LOC .NET Framework 4.7.2 monolith with WCF services, EF6, and ASP.NET Web Forms. We want to modernize to .NET 9. Should we use strangler fig or big bang migration?
cases.context: Active codebase, 20 developers, 15% test coverage, 8 WCF endpoints used by external partners, 2-year modernization budget

cases.input: We have 6 internal WCF services used by other .NET applications within our organization. We want to migrate them to modern protocols as part of our .NET 9 modernization. What's the approach?
cases.context: All consumers are internal .NET apps we control, no external consumers, some services have complex bindings

cases.input: Our application uses EF6 with lazy loading, EntitySQL queries, and EDMX models. We need to migrate to EF Core. What are the key risks and migration steps?
cases.context: .NET Framework 4.8 app, EF6.4, SQL Server backend, lazy loading used extensively, some EntitySQL queries in reports

cases.input: We have an ASP.NET Web Forms application with 150 pages, event-driven code-behind, and heavy use of ViewState. We want to modernize the UI as part of our .NET 9 migration. What's the recommended approach?
cases.context: Internal intranet application, 100 users, ASP.NET Web Forms 4.7, WebSocket support needed, moderate traffic

cases.input: We have a .NET Framework 4.5 application that cannot be fully migrated to .NET 8 in the next 12 months due to dependency on a vendor library with no .NET Core version. What are our options?
cases.context: Critical production app, vendor says .NET Core support is 18+ months away, containerization needed for cloud deployment
